result.markdown:
---
title: "Github Classroms - Revisted in 2026"
format: html
editor: visual
draft: true
---

I've made a few posts before about using GitHub Classrooms for teaching quant/comp courses.

-   [Some lessons in using Github Classroom and Codespaces](/posts/2024/02/2024-02-14_gh-classroom/) (February 2024)

-   [Troubleshooting RStudio for GitHub Classrom and Codespaces](/posts/2024/09/2024-09-06_rserver-codespaces/) (September 2024)

I've gotten a few more GH Classroom courses under my belt, so I thought I'd share a bit more about my workflow and configuration.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(distributional)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggdist)
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
r = 0.9
dist <- dist_multivariate_normal(
  mu = list(c(0,0)), 
  sigma = list(matrix(c(1,r,r,1), ncol=2)))
dimnames(dist) <- c("x", "y")

generate(dist, 10000) |> 
  pluck(1) |> 
  as_tibble() ->
  samp

samp |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(x, y)
  ) + 
  geom_point()+
  coord_fixed()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=768}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
samp |> 
  summarise(
    across(x:y, sd, .names = "{.col}_sd"),
    r = cor(x,y)
  )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 3
   x_sd  y_sd     r
  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
1 0.988 0.990 0.895
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
x <- c("kitten", "sitting", "knittig")
adist(x, x)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
     [,1] [,2] [,3]
[1,]    0    3    3
[2,]    3    0    3
[3,]    3    3    0
```


:::
:::
